title: The Maid Who Said Yes
narration: I am in Nazareth, the little town — the small place, not the great city.
Christ does not choose a wide kingdom for his nuptials. He chooses this.
And he sends to me not just any maiden.
Virgin in mind. Virgin in body. Virgin in vow.
In my soul I have made it: I will know no man. I am sealed with that promise.
Then he comes — Gabriel. The strength of God, his name says.
An archangel, sent for a great announcing.
And I am troubled. Perplexed.
For Ambrose is right of me: I dare not disbelieve an angel, yet I dare not too rashly seize the things of God.
He tells me I will conceive, will bear a son.
This one will be great — not great as a man before the Lord, but great as God, the Son of God.
And the question rises out of my fear — for one thing I fear above all, that my virginity be lost.
So I ask of the manner: How shall this be?
Since I know not a man — for in my soul I have vowed never to know one.
He does not break the seal. He fulfills it.
The Holy Spirit will overshadow. The power of the Most High alone.
I feel it begin — not a place changed, but a Presence made flesh within me.
From my own flesh he takes flesh. From my own members he fashions his.
Here Eve was deceived; here I am taught. There the loss; here the repair.
The flower conceived in the flower, of the flower, among the flowers.
And I am the handmaid. Let it be.
prayer: O Jesus, Son of the living God,
who by the Father's will and the Spirit's working
came down into the womb of a virgin,
bending low through the humble valleys to look upon your handmaid's lowliness —
let your grace come down, merciful Jesus,
through the merits of your Mother and Virgin, upon me, your most unworthy servant,
that through her I may desire you, and inwardly conceive you in love,
and bring forth the saving fruit of good works. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Quomodo fiet istud, quoniam virum non cognosco?
latinJewel.english: How shall this be, since I know not a man?
